visuals.alt: Conceptual Mobazha product map showing storefronts, community channels, and Agent integrations connected to an operator-selected backend, with payment, delivery, discovery, and hosted services as explicit dependencies.
visuals.caption: One commerce core can support several operating and distribution paths without moving business-state authority into the presentation channel.
visuals.claim: Explains the product and authority model; it does not assert that every pictured channel, integration, or external service is available in every distribution.

visuals.alt: Conceptual model separating an access account or credential, the store and Node business-state boundary, storefront views that share store state, and the channels that present those views.
visuals.caption: Accounts authorize access, stores own business state, storefronts shape presentation, and channels provide entry context.
visuals.claim: Explains product and authority boundaries; it does not assert that every distribution enables multiple stores, named storefronts, every channel, or future identity models.

visuals.alt: Conceptual transaction spine showing a normal path from reviewed terms through order creation, payment verification, fulfillment, and completion, with separate bands for order authority, payment evidence, and policy-gated recovery.
visuals.caption: One trade connects order, payment, and protection state without collapsing them into one misleading status.
visuals.claim: Explains durable authority and recovery boundaries; it does not assert that every distribution, order, payment rail, or protection model exposes every pictured action.

visuals.alt: Conceptual buyer journey from reviewing one seller quote through payment, fulfillment, and completion, with recovery actions dependent on active order state.
visuals.caption: One seller-owned order, from reviewed quote to observable completion.
visuals.claim: Explains the documentation authority and milestone model; it does not assert that every deployment exposes every transition.

visuals.alt: Conceptual seller operating loop covering store identity and policy, listings, payment readiness, a test purchase, and ongoing order operations.
visuals.caption: A store is ready only when the complete buyer journey has been tested.
visuals.claim: Explains the operator responsibility sequence; it is not evidence that a specific store has completed these checks.

visuals.alt: Conceptual self-hosting boundary showing clients entering through an operator-controlled network boundary to a Mobazha Node and local data, with payment systems and optional hosted services remaining separate dependencies.
visuals.caption: Self-hosting moves operational control to the Node operator; external dependencies remain explicit.
visuals.claim: Explains responsibility boundaries and does not prescribe one mandatory network topology.
